entities.description: bookrag is a system that utilizes specific operators to answer queries and prune search spaces
figure 8 is an illustration depicting bookrag s answering workflow across different query types
single hop refers to a type of query case where the reasoning space is reduced from 134 to 24 nodes
multi hop is a type of query case handled by bookrag s answering workflow
global queries are a type of query case processed by bookrag s answering workflow
select is a specific operator leveraged by bookrag to prune search spaces
decompose is a specific operator leveraged by bookrag to prune search spaces
filter is a specific operator leveraged by bookrag to prune search spaces
134 represents the initial number of nodes in the reasoning space for the single hop case
24 represents the reduced number of nodes in the reasoning space for the single hop case
case study is the context or type of analysis being presented in the text
answering workflow is the process illustrated by figure 8 that bookrag uses to handle queries
search spaces are the areas that bookrag prunes using specific operators to improve efficiency
relevant evidence is the specific information that bookrag isolates from noise
noise refers to irrelevant data from which bookrag isolates relevant evidence
precise answer generation is the outcome ensured by bookrag s ability to isolate relevant evidence

relations.description: figure 8 illustrates the answering workflow of bookrag
bookrag processes single hop queries  reducing the reasoning space significantly
bookrag processes multi hop queries as part of its answering workflow
bookrag processes global queries as part of its answering workflow
bookrag leverages the select operator to prune search spaces
bookrag leverages the decompose operator to prune search spaces
bookrag leverages the filter operator to prune search spaces
the single hop case starts with a reasoning space of 134 nodes
the single hop case reduces the reasoning space to 24 nodes
